L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the image resampling logic. When processing images with attacker-controlled dimensions, certain size parameters are not validated before being used in arithmetic operations for memory allocation. This can result in a heap-based buffer overflow, potentially allowing remote attackers to cause a denial of service or execute arbitrary code via crafted image dimensions.

L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the image resampling logic. When processing images with attacker-controlled dimensions, certain size parameters are not validated before being used in arithmetic operations for memory allocation. This can result in a heap-based buffer overflow, potentially allowing remote attackers to cause a denial of service or execute arbitrary code via crafted image dimensions.

ground_truth.reason: An integer overflow in the horizontal image resampling function allows negative or very large size values to bypass allocation size calculations, leading to a heap-based buffer overflow when writing resampled pixel data.
